reason: Drive remaining CLAUDE.md expanded KG checklist items to validated completion with bounded scratch-first work.

CLAUDE.md updated for promoted biosample/expression GCS state: 8/15 node files, 23/77 edge files, 3,199,166 nodes, 24,940,044 edges, zero dangling edges; cell_type and expression relations removed from missing lists.

Selected and merged OpenTargets disease_phenotype after patching MONDO/HP normalization and endpoint stubs. Promoted disease, phenotype, and disease_has_phenotype Parquets to GCS. Final GCS validate PASS: 3,203,188 nodes, 25,030,503 edges, total_dangling_edges=0.
CLAUDE.md Phase 9 now specifies final TxGNN model smoke constraints: tiny model, systemd CPUQuota=200%, MemoryMax=4G, local/scratch data only, no production GCS mutation, pressure treated as blocker.

Merged OpenTargets pharmacogenomics. Promoted nodes/mutation.parquet, updated nodes/molecule.parquet, and edges/mutation_affects_molecule_response.parquet. GCS validate PASS: 3,205,619 nodes, 25,035,369 edges, total_dangling_edges=0. Coverage now 9/15 node files and 24/77 edge files.

Merged cached OpenTargets drug_molecule into nodes/molecule.parquet. Added/enriched 22,230 ChEMBL records with drug_type/is_approved/inchikey/smiles and 19,100 DrugBank IDs; row count remains 31,007 because endpoints already existed. GCS validate PASS: 3,205,619 nodes, 25,035,369 edges, total_dangling_edges=0, peak RSS about 6.2G.
GO scratch ingest still too slow after schema/chunking fix; stopped before validation/promotion

Recovered and merged OpenTargets target+GO. Optimized dedup_edges fast path for no-evidence edge tables; pathway_contains_gene write dropped from stuck CPU loop to 8.6s. Promoted nodes/gene.parquet, nodes/pathway.parquet, and edges/pathway_contains_gene.parquet. GCS validate PASS: 3,233,849 nodes, 25,325,918 edges, total_dangling_edges=0, peak RSS about 6.3G.

Selected next safe work item after target+GO: resolve legacy protein endpoint conflation before running interaction/MoA/protein-node exports. Current GCS protein-named relations use gene endpoints, so blindly ingesting interaction would compound semantic mismatch.

Audited all protein-named GCS edge files. They physically use gene endpoints: protein_interacts_protein gene→gene, and molecule_targets_protein/disease_associated_protein/pathway_contains_protein/tissue_expresses_protein/phenotype_associated_protein y_type=gene. Updated CLAUDE.md to mark them as legacy gene/protein-conflated relations and to block blind interaction/MoA ingestion until protein node/remapping is handled.

Applied Jérémie's schema decision: protein primary IDs are Ensembl Protein ENSP, not UniProt. Updated manage_db/kg_schema.py NodeType.PROTEIN primary ontology/id_format/example/xrefs and CLAUDE.md node schema/protein-conflation note. Tests passed: tests/test_kg_storage.py and tests/test_credibility.py.

Implemented ENSP protein node export from OpenTargets target.transcripts.translationId. Promoted nodes/protein.parquet with 233,995 ENSP protein nodes and UniProt/gene xrefs. GCS validate PASS: 10 node types, 24 edge types, 3,467,844 nodes, 25,325,918 edges, total_dangling_edges=0, peak RSS about 6.5G.

Audited legacy protein-edge remapping coverage against ENSP protein nodes. Direct blind remapping is unsafe: most legacy endpoints are NCBI IDs, and many genes have multiple ENSP translations. Only 1,528 molecule_targets_protein endpoints were unambiguously single-ENSP. Updated CLAUDE.md: next safe edge is gene_encodes_protein from nodes/protein.parquet, not rewriting legacy protein-named edges.

Exported edges/gene_encodes_protein.parquet from protein.ensembl_gene_id to ENSP IDs. Promoted 233,995 ENSG→ENSP edges to GCS. Final GCS validate PASS: 10 node types, 25 edge types, 3,467,844 nodes, 25,559,913 edges, total_dangling_edges=0.

Implemented transcript node export from OpenTargets target transcripts. Promoted nodes/transcript.parquet (507,365 ENST nodes), edges/gene_has_transcript.parquet (507,365), and edges/transcript_encodes_protein.parquet (233,995). Final GCS validate PASS: 11 node types, 27 edge types, 3,975,209 nodes, 26,301,273 edges, total_dangling_edges=0.

Planned OpenTargets variants merge. Staged OT 26.03 variant locally (25 files, 3.2G, 7,432,549 rows). Patched ingest_variants to use mutation schema hgvs/clinvar_id/gnomad_id, map protein-change edges to ENSP through unambiguous UniProt xrefs, and lower variant batch size. One-file smoke passed: 311,562 mutation nodes, 11,652,040 mutation_in_gene, 11,652,040 mutation_affects_transcript, 8,563 mutation_causes_protein_change, peak RSS 6.5G. Decision: do not blindly promote full gene/transcript variant relations; next bounded slice is mutation_causes_protein_change ENSP only.
failed historical task superseded by completed recovery task 7
bounded variant protein-change/GWAS safe slice completed in later agent pass
Complete remaining KG/LaminDB blockers and final smoke
Continue storage cleanup for remaining local TxGNN data

Storage cleanup pass completed: repo-migrated legacy TxGNN agent reports; archived+verified+removed four local scratch directories (bounded-promotion, drug-molecule, known-variants, gwas-join) under /mnt/gcs/jouvencekb/kg/local-archive/home-ubuntu-data-txgnn-20260611T0907Z; updated docs/prompts away from removed /home paths; created follow-up task for remaining local TxGNN data because txgnn-literature GCS FUSE copy stalled and was preserved.

Archived and verified all remaining TxGNN /home/ubuntu/data directories into /mnt/gcs/jouvencekb/kg/local-archive/home-ubuntu-data-txgnn-20260611T0940Z as deterministic .tar.zst files with sha256 manifest and zstd -t checks; removed local sources; find /home/ubuntu/data -maxdepth 1 -type d -name 'txgnn*' now returns none; updated CLAUDE.md, docs/storage_cleanup.md, docs/kg_lamindb_blockers.md, active OMOC prompts, memory, and final report .omoc/reports/txgnn-ralph-loop-20260611T0958Z.final.

Fixed LaminDB cloud path/parity task: sync now skips stale ln.connect hub refresh for already-current jkobject/jouvencekb, preventing wrong gs://jouvencekb/.lamindb/lamin.db lookup; repaired canonical /mnt/gcs/jouvencekb/kg/v2 registry parity for paper/mutation/protein without /home/ubuntu/data by creating 30,851 Paper and 1,456 Mutation records and handling gnomAD-like mutation node IDs. Verified targeted parity missing_ids=0 for paper, mutation, protein; tests passed (21). Full KG validation was not rerun to completion because no Parquet changed and a fresh attempt was CPU-bound/no-output after ~22 min.
Continue non-disease KG coverage and final smoke

Resolved the non-MONDO disease registry policy blocker as a bounded slice: deployed custom lnschema_txgnn.Disease migration 0005, changed disease sync/parity to use normalized source ontology IDs in the custom registry instead of direct bionty.Disease inserts, synced canonical GCS disease nodes, and verified disease parity missing_ids=0. Added follow-up tasks 22 (promote mutation_associated_disease) and 23 (remaining KG coverage/final smoke); no /home/ubuntu/data use.

Promoted mutation_associated_disease to canonical /mnt/gcs/jouvencekb/kg/v2 from archived known-variant and GWAS scratch without /home/ubuntu/data. Added 4,598 disease node rows (52,889 total) and 4,656,171 mutation->disease edges; targeted endpoint validation reports missing_mutations=0, missing_diseases=0, bad types/relations=0. Synced 3,536 additional custom lnschema_txgnn.Disease records; parity audit reports missing_ids=0 for canonical disease and mutation. Coverage now 11/15 node files, 30/77 edge files, 6,566,887 nodes, 31,670,272 edges. Tests passed: 26. Fresh full streaming validator attempt timed out after 20m with no output on GCS FUSE, so completion evidence relies on prior clean full canonical validation plus targeted validation of the only new edge and node delta.
Plan remaining KG exports then final smoke

Completed a bounded validation slice for remaining KG work: added manage_db.audit_node_ontology_coverage with tests, ran it on canonical /mnt/gcs/jouvencekb/kg/v2, and saved .omoc/reports/node-ontology-coverage-20260611T112539Z.json. Evidence: 6,566,887 node rows across 11/15 node files; missing organism, cell_line, dataset, enhancer; namespace audit exposes underscore-form ontology IDs in disease/cell_type canonical Parquets plus legacy DrugBank/CTD/NCBI/gnomAD-like/OTVAR namespaces. Updated CLAUDE.md and docs/kg_coverage_audit.md, appended OMOC memory, and added follow-up tasks 25 (normalize ontology separators scratch-first, validate/parity) and 24 (remaining KG exports then final constrained smoke). Tests passed: 17 targeted.

Normalized canonical ontology ID separators scratch-first and promoted 12 affected disease/cell_type node/edge Parquets to /mnt/gcs/jouvencekb/kg/v2. Backed up originals under /mnt/gcs/jouvencekb/kg/local-archive/canonical-v2-pre-ontology-id-normalization-20260611T113134Z. Targeted canonical validation: missing_selected_endpoints=0, underscore_selected_endpoints=0, disease/cell_type underscore node IDs=0. Disease LaminDB parity missing_ids=0. Coverage now 11/15 node files, 30/77 edge files, 6,555,857 nodes, 31,670,272 edges; disease rows 41,859 after collapsing 11,030 duplicate normalized IDs. Added manage_db.normalize_ontology_id_separators and tests; targeted tests passed (28).
Repair remaining canonical LaminDB parity gaps

Completed bounded next KG export slice for task 24: promoted human organism schema-completion files to canonical /mnt/gcs/jouvencekb/kg/v2 without /home/ubuntu/data (nodes/organism NCBITaxon:9606; organism_has_gene 109,325; organism_has_tissue 16,061). Targeted scratch and canonical validation report zero dangling/bad endpoints; bionty organism parity missing_ids=0; coverage now 12/15 node files, 32/77 edge files, 6,555,858 nodes, 31,795,658 edges. Added export CLI/tests and updated CLAUDE/docs/memory/final report. Full parity audit exposed remaining registry gaps (transcript/gene/molecule/pathway/tissue/cell_type), so final smoke remains deferred; added follow-up OMOC task 26 to repair parity before final smoke.
Decide exact-ID policy for public registry parity gaps

Task 26 completed as a partial parity repair plus precise blocker: synced all 507,365 canonical transcript nodes into lnschema_txgnn.Transcript and verified transcript missing_ids=0. Added dry-run mapping for pathway/tissue/cell_type and fail-closed unsafe public bionty/pertdb --write paths after live evidence showed bionty.Gene and pertdb.Compound resolve by symbol/name and mutate existing IDs instead of exact KG primary IDs. Remaining blocked gaps pending Jérémie exact-ID registry policy: gene 30,604; molecule 21,672; pathway 1,566; tissue 291; cell_type 76. Tests passed: tests/test_sync_parquet_nodes_to_lamindb.py (15). Evidence and final report written under .omoc/reports; docs/kg_lamindb_blockers.md, CLAUDE.md, and OMOC memory updated; follow-up task 27 added.

Verified non-overlapping Ralph systemd loop: txgnn-ralph-loop.timer is enabled/active, service runs .omoc/bin/txgnn-ralph-loop.sh from the TxGNN repo, GCS mount gate is present, and direct second invocation while the current tick is active exits via flock with 'another tick is already running'. Documented docs/ralph_systemd_loop.md and wrote .omoc/reports/txgnn-ralph-loop-20260611T1215Z.final.
True blocker requiring Jérémie: exact-ID policy is required before safely repairing public bionty/pertdb-backed canonical parity gaps or running final smoke.
Run final TxGNN tiny smoke under systemd limits

Final tiny TxGNN smoke passed under systemd CPUQuota=200% and MemoryMax=4G. Evidence .omoc/reports/hermes-final-txgnn-tiny-smoke-20260612T182202Z.txt: service result success, runtime 7.235s, peak memory 449.1M, KGLoader mini KG ok with zero dangling edges, HeteroRGCN initialized on CPU, forward pass produced 6 positive and 6 negative relation score groups.

result: Resolved by Jérémie decision and Hermes verification: use custom TxGNN exact-ID registries for residual public bionty/pertdb-backed parity gaps (Gene, Molecule, Pathway, Tissue, CellType), not unsafe public-registry writes. Canonical audit reports missing_ids=0 for gene, molecule, pathway, tissue, cell_type, transcript, disease, protein, paper, mutation, and organism. Evidence: .omoc/reports/hermes-parity-custom-registries-*.json.
